channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Undertale Is Coming To Rift of the Necrodancer
item.content:encoded:
Get ready to have a bad time. Undertale is coming to Rift of the Necrodancer.

Announced during Thursday’s Triple I Initiative Showcase, the music from Undertale is coming to the rhythm title. Available starting today on Steam, songs from the game, including the classic Megalovania, will be front and center in your battles.

First released on Steam in February 2025 before coming to Nintendo Switch a month later, Rift of the Necrodancer sees you play a Cadence, who’s been brought into a strange new world. On your journey, you’ll engage in musical combat with monsters that all have unique behaviors to throw you off your groove.

Features of the game include music battles with scaling difficulty, global leaderboards, custom music, boss battles, and minigames. There’s also a Remix mode that mixes up the beatmaps on every playthrough to keep the game feeling fresh on subsequent runs.

As far as Undertale, it was released back in 2015. Since then, it has grown to be one of the most beloved titles of the last 10+ years. The game sees you play as a human who’s fallen into the underground and has to find their way out in a world full of monsters and dangers.

Are you going to check out the Undertale music pack in Rift of the Necrodancer? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, check out the newly released demo for Backyard Baseball ahead of its July launch.. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.title: SpaceCraft Early Access Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Shiro Games has announced the release date for SpaceCraft’s Early Access launch, and it is arriving in May 2026.

SpaceCraft is an upcoming title that space explorers will enjoy. Players will have the opportunity to check out different solar systems and planets, from which they can mine and craft resources. The title also includes the designing and building of ships, planetary bases, and much more.

SpaceCraft Early Access Launch Set for May 20

As announced at the latest Triple-i Initiative showcase, SpaceCraft will be available through Early Access from May 20. Interested readers can wishlist the game on Steam now. Along with the latest announcement, the developers also revealed a brand new trailer, which interested readers can check out below.

In SpaceCraft, humanity has been pushed to the brink of extinction by the reactivation of the Tripods. However, the people have received one last hope: to jump through a portal and explore and survive in an unseen galaxy. You will have your trusty spaceship as you visit all the new planets, maneuver through the asteroid fields, uncover secrets at abandoned space stations, and more.

The game is based on survival, so players will need to set down food on different planets to look for natural resources that not only help them survive but also serve as valuable materials for their ships and trade. Automation is a big deal in the game, where players can create fully automated resource hubs with production and delivery lines through drones and cargo ships.

The exploration sounds easy, but it isn’t, as players will need to keep an eye out for rogue factions like the Space Pirates. There are three stages of combat: Detection, Pursuit, and Engagement, with each stage testing the players’ tactical skill.

In other news, here’s everything that was announced at the Triple-i Initiative showcase. Will you be playing SpaceCraft once it launches in Early Access next month? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the new free-to-play Borderlands game. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: ‘On The Trail’ Free DLC Announced For Cairn
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to fill your backpack and load up on rope, as a series of free DLC has been announced for Cairn, with the first piece, On The Trail: Deep Water, arriving this Summer.

The official news comes from the Triple I Initiative 2026, with the gaming showcase’s third iteration featuring 40 games, with eight exclusive world premieres.

Check out the trailer below:

‘On The Trail—Deep Water’ Free DLC Arriving Summer 2026

Cairn was released to critical acclaim back in January this year, and now, fans of the survival climber have an excuse to pursue even more daunting peaks, thanks to the upcoming waves of free DLC. The first of the bunch, Deep Water, is set to release this Summer, and it’s filled to the brim with new content.

You’ll be following the trail of a legendary climber as you explore three new climbing areas, each with its own set of challenges to navigate. Additionally, if the heights are a cause for concern, worry not, as you’ll be able to water solo: it’ll be possible to climb over water, thus alleviating the fear of falling to your death!

And for those of you who felt isolated on your hefty climbs, there will be the added bonus of climbing alongside Marco or Aava, making the ascents slightly less lonely.

This marks just the beginning for the free DLC The Game Bakers has planned for Cairn, with more to follow throughout the year. It’s safe to assume there will be even more perilous peaks to scale, so put on your harness and buckle up!

Will you be diving into Cairn’s free DLC? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more announcements, check out everything revealed at the Triple I Initiative Showcase.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: New Gameplay Revealed of Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse
item.content:encoded:
New gameplay footage has been shown from Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse.

During the Triple-i Initiative Showcase on April 9, new gameplay of the upcoming Castlevania game was shown. The gameplay showed more 2D action from the upcoming title. In this game, players follow Trevor Belmont, the last of the Belmont bloodline. The title was first revealed by Konami during the February Sony PlayStation State of Play presentation.

Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse Setting Revealed

Alongside the new gameplay, the developer provided commentary that highlighted the game’s mix of action and exploration. Additionally, the game’s setting was revealed.

Belmont’s Curse will be taking place in Paris in 1499. The new game is also set 23 years after Castlevania: Dracula’s Curse. Dracula’s Curse was the third game in the franchise, released in 1989 for the Nintendo Entertainment System.

In February, the game was announced as the first release in the celebration of 40 years of Castlevania. The original Castlevania game debuted in 1986 for the Famicom Disk System and was released in America on the NES in 1987.

No release date has been announced for Belmont’s Curse, but it is expected to launch this year. Insider Gaming will provide updates on the latest announcements regarding this upcoming game in the legendary franchise.

For more updates on Konami from Insider Gaming, check out the latest news about the company increasing employee base salaries and its focus on the future.

Check out everything being announced during the Triple-i Initiative Showcase at this link.

Are you excited for the return of the legendary series? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Prove You’re Human Announced At Triple I Initiative 2026
item.content:encoded:
In 2026, all any wonders anymore is if something is AI or not. That question is most certainly posed in sunset vistor’s Prove You’re Human, which has been revealed to the world during the newest edition of the Triple I Initiative.

Another year, another glorious Triple I Initiative showcase. The event casts a special spotlight on indie games, not the big-money AAA titans of the world. If you didn’t know, the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28 will also do something similar.

Until then, it’s time to focus on one indie game, in particular, and that’s Prove You’re Human.

Can You Prove You’re Human?

The premise of the newly announced Prove Your Human is simple: ‘Prove You’re Human splits the main character in two, and you drew the short straw. You’re the digital copy of a person who was paid to test a corporate product. But the product is convinced it’s as human as you are. Maybe even more.’

You need to connect with the AI, become acquainted with it, ironically learn about it (as AI likes to mimic humans), and get to the bottom of this AI struggle.

The first Prove You’re Human trailer, shown during Triple I Initiative 2026, is right here.

In a world rapidly becoming consumed by AI, Prove You’re Human is a scarily relatable entity.

Whatever your thoughts on AI, has the game caught your attention? Will you be keeping a watchful human eye on it? Share your thoughts on Prove You’re Human via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Until it comes out, spend some free time reading about SteamGPT reportedly being in the works for Support and CS2 Anti-Cheat.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: New Free-To-Play Borderlands Title Discovered
item.content:encoded:
A new free-to-play ‘Borderlands Mobile’ title has been discovered. It’s only for mobile devices, and even then, it’s quite restrictive, but the new entry in the franchise features the usual looting and shooting you’ve come to expect.

In one of the more bizarre discoveries, the existence of a new free-to-play Borderlands game, exclusive to mobile, has been found on the iOS store. The game page reveals the ‘price tag,’ some screenshots of the game, and its game file size.

Borderlands Mobile Is Exclusive To Apple Store

If you head to the App Store and search for it, you should find Borderlands Mobile.

The game is listed as free, and is only 2.9 GB to add to your phone—much more comforting than the 100+ GB that last year’s Borderlands 4 required on the various formats!

Unfortunately, Borderlands Mobile can ONLY be found on iOS, as Android users have to miss out, as do console owners, if it wasn’t already clear.

Zynga Inc. is the developer, and the store description promises the ability to ‘Loot wild weapons,’ ‘blast enemies,’ and ‘join other Vault Hunters in an ever-evolving universe to fight greedy corporations and open legendary Vaults.’

Borderlands Mobile’s screenshots show popular characters like Claptrap and Moxxi, but not too much else is revealed on the surface. Obviously, a free-to-play Borderlands Mobile title is likely to come with its fair share of microtransactions.

Be warned, Vault Hunter.

Is it weird that Take-Two hasn’t advertised or officially announced Borderlands Mobile? Will you be downloading it and giving it a go? Let us know via the free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, make sure you catch up on everything that happened during the Triple-I Initiative Showcase 2026, as well as SteamGPT reportedly being in the works for support and CS2 Anti-Cheat.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…
